name: Toberia Waterfalls Trail
name_original: Cascadas de la Toberia desde Andoin
description: A difficult 2.7-mile (4.4 km) jogging trail through lush beech forests to the impressive Tobería Waterfalls, gaining 1070 feet.
region_intro: Jogging around Asparrena offers diverse terrain within the historical territory of Álava, characterized by green, hilly landscapes of the Gorbea Foothills and the forested Alavese Mountains. The region features the impressive karst landscape of Aizkorri-Aratz Natural Park, providing varied running experiences from gentle slopes to challenging uphill paths. Natural highlights include the Source of the River Zirauntza and routes through lush beech forests leading to the Toberia Waterfalls. This environment provides a stimulating setting for runners seeking natural beauty and varied physical challenges. Best jogging routes around Asparrena The most popular jogging route is Toberia Waterfalls Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes about 50 minutes to complete. This path winds through lush beech forests and leads to the impressive Tobería Waterfalls. Another top favourite among local runners is Collado de Elorrola – Hermitage of Andra Mari de Urbia loop from Komentua, a difficult 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This route offers scenic views as it traverses mountain terrain towards a historic hermitage. Local runners also love the Hermitage of the Holy Spirit – San Adrián Tunnel loop from San Adriango tunela, a 5.1 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes and a notable tunnel, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Jogging around Asparrena is defined by karst landscapes, river sources, and beech forests. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Asparrena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 1200 runners have used komoot to explore Asparrena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Asparrena?
A: Asparrena offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 120 trails available for you to explore. These routes cater to various preferences, from gentle jogs to more challenging mountain runs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Asparrena?
A: The terrain in Asparrena is quite diverse, ranging from the green, hilly landscapes of the Gorbea Foothills to the more challenging, forested Alavese Mountains. You'll find paths winding through lush beech forests, impressive karst landscapes within the Aizkorri-Aratz Natural Park, and serene trails along river sources like the Zirauntza. This variety ensures stimulating experiences for all types of runners.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of running routes in Asparrena?
A: Asparrena provides a good mix of difficulty levels. While most of the routes are considered difficult (around 93), there are also 27 moderate trails and at least one easy route available. This means you can find options whether you're looking for a challenging workout or a more relaxed run.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see along the jogging routes in Asparrena?
A: Yes, Asparrena is home to beautiful waterfalls that you can discover while jogging. A popular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058957622/toberia-waterfalls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toberia Waterfalls Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through beech forests to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3080140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobería Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3472430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andoin Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4156267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urkate Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; in the region.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I explore while running in Asparrena?
A: Beyond waterfalls, Asparrena's running routes offer access to several notable natural features and landmarks. You can explore the impressive karst landscape of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5027459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aizkorri-Aratz Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, visit the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5027459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the River Zirauntza&lt;/a&gt;, or even pass through the notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10524046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián Tunnel&lt;/a&gt; on certain trails. The region also features pilgrimage routes like the Camino Ignaciano, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Asparrena?
A: The running trails in Asparrena are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 1200 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the natural beauty, diverse landscapes, and the stimulating challenge of the routes.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in Asparrena?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Asparrena are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24647356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Elorrola – Hermitage of Andra Mari de Urbia loop from Komentua&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15005639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Zirauntza River loop from Araia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging options in Asparrena?
A: While many routes in Asparrena are more challenging, there are options suitable for families looking for a gentler run. Look for routes described as 'easy' or those that follow flatter sections along river paths, such as parts of the trails near the Source of the River Zirauntza, which offer pleasant, less strenuous terrain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Asparrena?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in the Basque Country, including Asparrena, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always best to check specific regulations for natural parks like Aizkorri-Aratz, as rules can vary. Always ensure your dog is under control and that you clean up after them.

Q: How can I access the jogging routes in Asparrena by public transport?
A: Asparrena's administrative center, Araia, serves as a hub and may offer public transport connections to nearby towns. For specific route access, it's advisable to check local bus schedules or regional transport services that connect to the villages within Asparrena, as some trailheads might be accessible from these points. Planning your route in advance will help identify the closest public transport stops.

Q: Where can I park when visiting the running trails in Asparrena?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many popular starting points for trails, especially near villages like Araia or significant natural attractions, will have designated parking areas. For routes like those leading to the Tobería Waterfalls or the Source of the Zirauntza River, you can often find parking close to the trail's beginning. It's recommended to check the specific route details on komoot for parking information.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Asparrena?
A: Asparrena offers great running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with lush greenery in spring and beautiful foliage in autumn. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are ideal. Winter offers a different charm, especially in the forested areas, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially wet or snowy conditions at higher elevations.
tips: In times of rain, the road is a real mud, but for example now in spring, you can go up a path that runs parallel to the river, you cross the river through a lock that has no major complication
Rocky ledge overlooking the plain and the peaks that surround Legaire.

faqs.question: Where does the Toberia Waterfalls Trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Komoot route for the Toberia Waterfalls Trail starts from Andoin. While specific parking details aren't provided, trailheads in this region typically have nearby parking facilities. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The Komoot route for this trail is approximately 4.4 kilometers (2.7 miles) long. It's categorized as 'difficult' for jogging, with an elevation gain of about 326 meters (1070 feet). Depending on your fitness level and the trail conditions, it could take around 50 minutes to an hour to complete.

faqs.question: Is the Toberia Waterfalls Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Komoot route for this trail is rated as 'difficult' for jogging due to its elevation gain and potentially challenging terrain. While some sections might be easier, beginners should be prepared for uneven and possibly muddy paths, especially after rain. There are 'Easy' routes in the area, but this specific jogging route requires good fitness and sure-footedness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies significantly. You'll find sections with mostly accessible paths, but other parts can be challenging, requiring sturdy shoes and sure-footedness. The trail winds through ancient beech and oak forests. Be aware that it can become extremely muddy and uncomfortable for running after rain, making a parallel path along the river a better option during spring.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the waterfalls?
faqs.answer: The waterfalls are at their most spectacular and vibrant after a rainy period, especially during the rainy season when you can see numerous water jumps along the river. This creates a truly magical landscape with abundant greenery. However, be prepared for muddy and more challenging trail conditions during these times.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is renowned for its beautiful scenery, particularly the series of waterfalls nestled within a lush beech forest. The main highlight is the Tobería Waterfalls, where the Arnoia river cascades down in two distinct drops, totaling 20 meters. You'll also pass by the La Toberia Viewpoint, offering great views.

faqs.question: Is the Toberia Waterfalls Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas where dogs are generally welcome, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety and comfort of other trail users. Be mindful of the challenging terrain and potential mud, which might be difficult for some pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to access the Toberia Waterfalls Trail. It is generally open to the public.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and potential for mud, you should bring sturdy trail running shoes with good grip. Hydration is essential, so carry enough water. Depending on the weather, layers of clothing are recommended. A small backpack for essentials and perhaps a light snack would also be useful.

faqs.question: Are there any specific tips for jogging the Toberia Waterfalls Trail after rain?
faqs.answer: Yes, after rain, the trail can become extremely muddy and uncomfortable for running. An insider tip is to consider using a path parallel to the river, especially during spring, as the main trail might be particularly challenging. Always prioritize safety and be prepared for slippery conditions.

faqs.question: Is the trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Komoot route provided is an out-and-back trail. However, research indicates that there are also loop options in the area, such as the Andoin Waterfall – Tobería Waterfalls loop from Venta de Egino, which is categorized as a Moderate hike.
